अयोनिर्योनिनिलया कूटस्था कुलरूपिणी ॥ १६६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvagrāsā vidrumābhā vaiṣṇavī viṣṇurūpiṇī .
ayoniryoninilayā kūṭasthā kularūpiṇī .. 166 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She devours the universe, shines like coral, and is Vaishnavi and the very form of Vishnu. Though without a womb, She resides in the womb; She is the immutable and essence of the family (kula).

contentBlocks.original:
वीरगोष्ठीप्रिया वीरा नैष्कर्म्या नादरूपिणी ।
विज्ञानकलना कल्या विदग्धा बैन्दवासना ॥ १६७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vīragoṣṭhīpriyā vīrā naiṣkarmyā nādarūpiṇī .
vijñānakalanā kalyā vidagdhā baindavāsanā .. 167 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She delights in the company of heroes, is Herself heroic, transcends action, and is the essence of sound. Skilled in realization and subtle knowledge, She is graceful, wise, and dwells in the bindu (causal point).

contentBlocks.original:
तत्त्वाधिका तत्त्वमयी तत्त्वमर्थ-स्वरूपिणी ।
सामगानप्रिया सौम्या सदाशिव-कुटुम्बिनी ॥ १६८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tattvādhikā tattvamayī tattvamartha-svarūpiṇī .
sāmagānapriyā saumyā sadāśiva-kuṭumbinī .. 168 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She transcends all principles, is made of tattva (truth), and is the essence of tattva's meaning. She loves the songs of the Sāma Ved, is gentle, and is the consort of Sadāśiva.

contentBlocks.original:
(सोम्या)
सव्यापसव्य-मार्गस्था सर्वापद्विनिवारिणी ।
स्वस्था स्वभावमधुरा धीरा धीरसमर्चिता ॥ १६९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
(somyā)
savyāpasavya-mārgasthā sarvāpadvinivāriṇī .
svasthā svabhāvamadhurā dhīrā dhīrasamarcitā .. 169 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is accessible through both right-hand and left-hand paths, removes all dangers, abides in Herself, is naturally sweet, courageous, and worshipped by the wise.

contentBlocks.original:
चैतन्यार्घ्य-समाराध्या चैतन्य-कुसुमप्रिया ।
सदोदिता सदातुष्टा तरुणादित्य-पाटला ॥ १७० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
caitanyārghya-samārādhyā caitanya-kusumapriyā .
sadoditā sadātuṣṭā taruṇāditya-pāṭalā .. 170 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is worshipped with the libation of pure consciousness and loves the flowers of consciousness. Always radiant and ever content, She glows with the hue of the rising sun.

contentBlocks.original:
दक्षिणा-दक्षिणाराध्या दरस्मेर-मुखाम्बुजा ।
कौलिनी-केवलाऽनर्घ्य-कैवल्य-पददायिनी ॥ १७१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dakṣiṇā-dakṣiṇārādhyā darasmera-mukhāmbujā .
kaulinī-kevalā'narghya-kaivalya-padadāyinī .. 171 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is Dakṣiṇā, worshipped through the Dakṣiṇa path, with a gentle, smiling lotus-like face. A pure Kaula practitioner, She grants the invaluable state of liberation (Kaivalya).

contentBlocks.original:
स्तोत्रप्रिया स्तुतिमती श्रुति-संस्तुत-वैभवा ।
मनस्विनी मानवती महेशी मङ्गलाकृतिः ॥ १७२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stotrapriyā stutimatī śruti-saṃstuta-vaibhavā .
manasvinī mānavatī maheśī maṅgalākṛtiḥ .. 172 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Lover of hymns, full of praises, Her glory is extolled in the Vedas. She is intelligent, noble, the consort of Śhiva, and the embodiment of auspiciousness.

contentBlocks.original:
विश्वमाता जगद्धात्री विशालाक्षी विरागिणी ।
प्रगल्भा परमोदारा परामोदा मनोमयी ॥ १७३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viśvamātā jagaddhātrī viśālākṣī virāgiṇī .
pragalbhā paramodārā parāmodā manomayī .. 173 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Mother of the universe, supporter of all, She has large eyes and is detached. Confident and supremely generous, She is full of supreme joy and manifests the mind.

contentBlocks.original:
व्योमकेशी विमानस्था वज्रिणी वामकेश्वरी ।
पञ्चयज्ञ-प्रिया पञ्च-प्रेत-मञ्चाधिशायिनी ॥ १७४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vyomakeśī vimānasthā vajriṇī vāmakeśvarī .
pañcayajña-priyā pañca-preta-mañcādhiśāyinī .. 174 ..
contentBlocks.translation: With the sky as her hair, residing in the celestial chariot, she is armed with a thunderbolt and is Vāmakeśvarī. Fond of the five yajñas, She reclines on a throne of five corpses.

contentBlocks.original:
पञ्चमी पञ्चभूतेशी पञ्च-संख्योपचारिणी ।
शाश्वती शाश्वतैश्वर्या शर्मदा शम्भुमोहिनी ॥ १७५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pañcamī pañcabhūteśī pañca-saṃkhyopacāriṇī .
śāśvatī śāśvataiśvaryā śarmadā śambhumohinī .. 175 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is the fifth (tithi), ruler of the five elements, and worshipped with five-fold offerings. Eternal, with eternal sovereignty, She grants happiness and enchants even Śhiva.

contentBlocks.original:
धराधरसुता धन्या धर्मिणी धर्मवर्धिनी ।
लोकातीता गुणातीता सर्वातीता शमात्मिका ॥ १७६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dharādharasutā dhanyā dharmiṇī dharmavardhinī .
lokātītā guṇātītā sarvātītā śamātmikā .. 176 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is Earth, daughter of the mountain, blessed, righteous, and promoter of dharma. Transcending world, qualities, and all else, She is the very essence of peace.

contentBlocks.original:
बन्धूक-कुसुमप्रख्या बाला लीलाविनोदिनी ।
सुमङ्गली सुखकरी सुवेषाढ्या सुवासिनी ॥ १७७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bandhūka-kusumaprakhyā bālā līlāvinodinī .
sumaṅgalī sukhakarī suveṣāḍhyā suvāsinī .. 177 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Resplendent like the bandhūka flower, She is ever young and playful. Bestower of auspiciousness and joy, richly adorned, and pleasing in appearance.

contentBlocks.original:
सुवासिन्यर्चन-प्रीताऽऽशोभना शुद्धमानसा ।
बिन्दु-तर्पण-सन्तुष्टा पूर्वजा त्रिपुराम्बिका ॥ १७८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvāsinyarcana-prītā''śobhanā śuddhamānasā .
bindu-tarpaṇa-santuṣṭā pūrvajā tripurāmbikā .. 178 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Delighted by worship from married women, She is radiant and pure-minded. Pleased by offerings to the Bindu, She is the primal mother, Tripurāmbikā.

contentBlocks.original:
दशमुद्रा-समाराध्या त्रिपुराश्री-वशङ्करी ।
ज्ञानमुद्रा ज्ञानगम्या ज्ञानज्ञेय-स्वरूपिणी ॥ १७९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
daśamudrā-samārādhyā tripurāśrī-vaśaṅkarī .
jñānamudrā jñānagamyā jñānajñeya-svarūpiṇī .. 179 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Worshipped with the ten Mudrās, She controls the glory of Tripurā. The gesture and path of wisdom, She is both knowledge and the known.

contentBlocks.original:
योनिमुद्रा त्रिखण्डेशी त्रिगुणाम्बा त्रिकोणगा ।
अनघाऽद्भुत-चारित्रा वाञ्छितार्थ-प्रदायिनी ॥ १८० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yonimudrā trikhaṇḍeśī triguṇāmbā trikoṇagā .
anaghā'dbhuta-cāritrā vāñchitārtha-pradāyinī .. 180 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is the Yoni Mudrā, goddess of the three divisions, mother of the three Guṇas, residing in the triangle. Pure, with wondrous conduct, She grants all desired fruits.

contentBlocks.original:
अभ्यासातिशय-ज्ञाता षडध्वातीत-रूपिणी ।
अव्याज-करुणा-मूर्तिरज्ञान-ध्वान्त-दीपिका ॥ १८१ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
abhyāsātiśaya-jñātā ṣaḍadhvātīta-rūpiṇī .
avyāja-karuṇā-mūrtirajñāna-dhvānta-dīpikā .. 181 ..
contentBlocks.translation: Mastered through intense practice, transcending the six paths, She is the embodiment of unconditional compassion and the light that dispels the darkness of ignorance.

contentBlocks.original:
आबाल-गोप-विदिता सर्वानुल्लङ्घ्य-शासना ।
श्रीचक्रराज-निलया श्रीमत्-त्रिपुरसुन्दरी ॥ १८२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ābāla-gopa-viditā sarvānullaṅghya-śāsanā .
śrīcakrarāja-nilayā śrīmat-tripurasundarī .. 182 ..
contentBlocks.translation: She is known and accessible even to the youngest — from infants to cowherd boys. Her command is supreme and cannot be transgressed by anyone. She resides in the majestic Śrīcakra and is revered as Śrīmat Tripurasundarī, the auspicious and beautiful goddess of the three cities.

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशिवा शिव-शक्त्यैक्य-रूपिणी ललिताम्बिका ।
एवं श्रीललिता देव्या नाम्नां साहस्रकं जगुः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśivā śiva-śaktyaikya-rūpiṇī lalitāmbikā .
evaṃ śrīlalitā devyā nāmnāṃ sāhasrakaṃ jaguḥ ..
contentBlocks.translation:
The auspicious Shiva and Shiva-Shakti united as one form, Lalita Ambika.
Thus the thousand names of the Goddess Shri Lalita were sung.

contentBlocks.original:
इति श्रीब्रह्माण्डपुराणे उत्तरखण्डे श्रीहयग्रीवागस्त्यसंवादे
श्रीललिता सहस्रनाम स्तोत्र कथनं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti śrībrahmāṇḍapurāṇe uttarakhaṇḍe śrīhayagrīvāgastyasaṃvāde
śrīlalitā sahasranāma stotra kathanaṃ sampūrṇam ..
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the narration of the Śrī Lalitā Sahasranāma Stotram — the hymn of the thousand names of Śrī Lalitā — from the Uttarakhaṇḍa of the Śrī Brahmāṇḍa Purāṇa, as recounted in the sacred dialogue between Śrī Hayagrīva and the sage Agastya.
